---
title: "Career Plan"
---

# Jesicca's Career Plan






# Jacky's Career Plan

## Apply Technical Skills More Directly to My Work

I already have a solid foundation in tools like Python, SQL, and data analysis, but the biggest opportunity is applying them more consistently to real business problems for clients.

Over the next few months, I plan to:

- Spend more time working with internal datasets to analyze pricing strategy in the marketplace and how it impacts leads per unit
- Rebuild or improve existing reports and dashboards to better understand how pricing, inventory, and performance metrics connect
- Start incorporating more advanced techniques like basic modeling or segmentation to identify patterns in pricing and lead performance

## Improve How I Translate Analysis Into Insights

A key gap for me is turning technical work into clear, actionable insights.

To improve this, I will focus on:

- Communicating findings in a simple and clear way
- Connecting analysis back to business outcomes instead of just reporting numbers
- Bringing more data-driven insights into conversations and decision-making

## Build Experience Through Projects and Collaboration

To keep developing, I need to consistently apply what I’m learning and make that work visible.

I plan to:

- Continue building hands-on projects using real datasets, including ones similar to the work I do at CarGurus
- Share these projects internally so other teams can use them and benefit from the insights
- Keep collaborating with my teammate to combine technical and business strengths

My focus is on strengthening technical skills, improving how I communicate insights, and continuing to build real, applied experience through projects and collaboration, with the goal of getting more involved in data-focused work within CarGurus over time.


# Jessica's Career Plan

My goal is to grow into a data analyst role by strengthening my technical skills, improving how I communicate insights, and gaining real-world experience. This plan outlines three steps I will take to bridge the gap between where I am now and where I want to be.

## Build More Technical Depth

While I have a good foundation in tools like Excel and some experience with Python and SQL, the biggest opportunity for me is to build more technical depth in those areas. 

To do that, I plan to: 

- Continue taking online courses at Boston University to deepen my understanding of Python, SQL, and data analysis techniques 
- Focus on building a portfolio of projects that demonstrate my technical abilities
- Pursue a certification in Python, SQL, or data analytics to validate and formalize my technical skills

## Improve Communication of Business Insights

Communication is one of the top skills listed in job postings for data analytics, so connecting analysis to clear business insights and storytelling is an important skill I want to continue to work on. 

To improve this, I will focus on:

- Practicing how to communicate findings in a simple and clear way that connects back to business outcomes
- Bringing more data-driven insights into conversations and decision-making at work
- Collaborating with my teammates in class and work to combine technical and business strengths in projects

## Build Experience and Professional Visibility

One of the best ways to grow as a data analyst is to apply skills in real-world settings beyond the classroom.

To do that, I plan to:

- Seek out internship or project opportunities where I can apply Python, SQL, and data analysis in a professional context
- Contribute to open-source or team-based data projects to build collaborative experience
- Document and share my work through GitHub and my personal portfolio to demonstrate growth to potential employers
